text.title: gcp-billing-cost Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gcp-billing-cost. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every production project MUST have a budget configured with alert thresholds at 50%, 90%, and 100% of the monthly spend target. Alerts MUST route to a monitored notification channel (email group or Pub/Sub).
rule.rationale: Without budget alerts, cost anomalies (runaway VMs, data egress spikes, accidental BigQuery full table scans) go undetected until the monthly bill. Multi-threshold alerts give early warning before overspend is irrecoverable.

rule.statement: Billing exports MUST be enabled to BigQuery for all production billing accounts. The export dataset must be queried via scheduled queries or Looker Studio to produce per-team cost reports at least monthly.
rule.rationale: The GCP console billing UI does not support custom cost allocation or chargeback. BigQuery exports are the only way to query costs by label, folder, or project combination at scale.

rule.statement: All projects MUST have env, team, and cost_center labels. All major resources (VMs, GKE node pools, Cloud SQL instances, buckets) MUST inherit or carry the same labels. Enforce via org policy custom constraint (custom.requireProjectLabels).
rule.rationale: Labels are propagated to billing export line items. A missing label on a VM means that VM's cost appears as unattributed in chargeback reports — the team is not charged and no one investigates the spend.
